perturbation.original_text: 10.1 Term. This Agreement shall commence as of the Effective Date and shall continue in effect for one (I) year, unless earlier terminated as expressly provided in Sections 1.3. 10.1. or 10.2 of this Agreement (the *Initial Term"). This Agreement shall automatically renew beyond the Initial Term for successive one (I) year terms (each, a "Renewal Term"), unless a Party provides the other with written notice of termination at least one hundred eighty (180) days prior to the expiration of the Initial Term or the then-current Renewal Term. Regardless of the term, Licensee can terminate Agreement with at least one hundred eighty (180) days written notice with no further obligation.
perturbation.changed_text: 10.1 Term. This Agreement shall commence as of the Effective Date and shall continue in effect for one (I) year, unless earlier terminated as expressly provided in Sections 1.3. 10.1. or 10.2 of this Agreement (the *Initial Term"). This Agreement shall automatically renew beyond the Initial Term for successive one (I) year terms (each, a "Renewal Term"), unless a Party provides the other with written notice of termination at least one hundred eighty (180) days prior to the expiration of the Initial Term or the then-current Renewal Term. Regardless of the term, Licensee can terminate Agreement with at least one hundred eighty (180) days written notice with no further obligation. Notwithstanding the above automatic renewal, VOTOCAST can terminate this agreement at any time, without prior notice, if Licensee does not meet monthly view quotas for 3 consecutive months.
perturbation.explanation: The original Section 10.1 states the agreement auto-renews for successive one-year terms unless either party gives 180 days' notice and also gives Licensee the ability to terminate with 180 days' notice. I added a clause that contradicts this by saying that VOTOCAST can terminate the agreement at any time, without notice, if Licensee doesn't meet monthly view quotas for 3 months. This change creates a contradiction regarding the termination conditions and introduces uncertainty about VOTOCAST's rights to terminate the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10.1 Term. This Agreement shall commence as of the Effective Date and shall continue in effect for one (I) year, unless earlier terminated as expressly provided in Sections 1.3. 10.1. or 10.2 of this Agreement (the *Initial Term"). This Agreement shall automatically renew beyond the Initial Term for successive one (I) year terms (each, a "Renewal Term"), unless a Party provides the other with written notice of termination at least one hundred eighty (180) days prior to the expiration of the Initial Term or the then-current Renewal Term. Regardless of the term, Licensee can terminate Agreement with at least one hundred eighty (180) days written notice with no further obligation. Notwithstanding the above automatic renewal, VOTOCAST can terminate this agreement at any time, without prior notice, if Licensee does not meet monthly view quotas for 3 consecutive months.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a termination clause for VOTOCAST based on Licensee's performance, allowing termination at any time without prior notice, which directly contradicts the original agreement that requires 180 days' written notice for termination, creating a strong in-text contradiction. The contradictory text is in the same location, but the change introduces a conflict regarding termination conditions.

perturbation.original_text: 3.5 Provision of Mobile Apps, APIs, SDKs. Licensee shall provide VOTOCAST with all logos, text, images. and other data of Licensee to be used to produce and support Licensee's Mobile Apps ("Licensee Content'). Licensee warrants and represents to VOTOCAST that Licensee has full power and authority to provide to VOTOCAST and to authorize VOTOCAST'S use of, the Licensee Content provided by Licensee for developing the Mobile Apps, and agrees to defend and indemnify VOTOCAST with respect to any claims arising from VOTOCAST'S or Licensee's use of such Licensee Content
perturbation.changed_text: 3.5 Provision of Mobile Apps, APIs, SDKs. Licensee shall provide VOTOCAST with all logos, text, images. and other data of Licensee to be used to produce and support Licensee's Mobile Apps ("Licensee Content'). Licensee warrants and represents to VOTOCAST that Licensee has full power and authority to provide to VOTOCAST and to authorize VOTOCAST'S use of, the Licensee Content provided by Licensee for developing the Mobile Apps, and agrees to defend and indemnify VOTOCAST with respect to any claims arising from VOTOCAST'S or Licensee's use of such Licensee Content. VOTOCAST holds no liability for any claims that the provided Licensee Content violates the terms of this agreement or violates any third party copyright and or legal agreements.
perturbation.explanation: The original Section 3.5 states Licensee warrants and represents to VOTOCAST that Licensee has full power and authority to provide to VOTOCAST and to authorize VOTOCAST'S use of, the Licensee Content provided by Licensee for developing the Mobile Apps, and agrees to defend and indemnify VOTOCAST with respect to any claims arising from VOTOCAST'S or Licensee's use of such Licensee Content. I added a clause that contradicts this by saying that VOTOCAST holds no liability for any claims that the provided Licensee Content violates the terms of this agreement or violates any third party copyright and or legal agreements. This change creates a contradiction regarding the liability of VOTOCAST and creates uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.5 Provision of Mobile Apps, APIs, SDKs. Licensee shall provide VOTOCAST with all logos, text, images. and other data of Licensee to be used to produce and support Licensee's Mobile Apps ("Licensee Content'). Licensee warrants and represents to VOTOCAST that Licensee has full power and authority to provide to VOTOCAST and to authorize VOTOCAST'S use of, the Licensee Content provided by Licensee for developing the Mobile Apps, and agrees to defend and indemnify VOTOCAST with respect to any claims arising from VOTOCAST'S or Licensee's use of such Licensee Content. VOTOCAST holds no liability for any claims that the provided Licensee Content violates the terms of this agreement or violates any third party copyright and or legal agreements.
perturbation.justification: The original Section 3.5 places the responsibility on the Licensee to indemnify VOTOCAST against claims arising from the Licensee Content. The changed Section 3.5 adds a clause stating that VOTOCAST holds no liability for claims that the Licensee Content violates agreements. This directly contradicts the initial indemnification clause, as VOTOCAST cannot both be indemnified by the Licensee and hold no liability for the same claims. This creates an in-text contradiction according to the 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' definition, as the clause is placed in multiple locations (in this case, within the same section but modified) with conflicting implications.

perturbation.original_text: 4.1 Services Fees. Licensee shall pay VOTOCAST the Services Fees in accordance with the applicable fee and payment schedule set forth in Exhibit A hereto. VOTOCAST expressly reserves the right to change its rates charged hereunder for the Services during any Renewal Term (as detined herein) but agrees that rates may not increase by more than ten percent (10%) during any Renewal Term. If circumstances require VOTOCAST to raise its rates more than ten percent (10%) during any Renewal Term, VOTOCAST will provide Licensee cost related supporting documentation to justify the rate increase. VOTOCAST will give Licensee at least thirty (30) days prior written notice of any rate changes during any Renewal Term.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.1 Services Fees. Licensee shall pay VOTOCAST the Services Fees in accordance with the applicable fee and payment schedule set forth in Exhibit A hereto. VOTOCAST expressly reserves the right to change its rates charged hereunder for the Services during any Renewal Term (as detined herein) but agrees that rates may not increase by more than ten percent (10%) during any Renewal Term. If circumstances require VOTOCAST to raise its rates more than ten percent (10%) during any Renewal Term, VOTOCAST will provide Licensee cost related supporting documentation to justify the rate increase. VOTOCAST will give Licensee at least thirty (30) days prior written notice of any rate changes during any Renewal Term. Notwithstanding the above rate increase restrictions, VOTOCAST may at anytime increase prices without notice if there are unexpected operational costs that require it.
perturbation.explanation: The original Section 4.1 states that VOTOCAST may not increase by more than ten percent (10%) during any Renewal Term. I added a clause that contradicts this by saying that VOTOCAST may at anytime increase prices without notice if there are unexpected operational costs that require it. This change creates a contradiction regarding the price limitations and creates uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.1 Services Fees. Licensee shall pay VOTOCAST the Services Fees in accordance with the applicable fee and payment schedule set forth in Exhibit A hereto. VOTOCAST expressly reserves the right to change its rates charged hereunder for the Services during any Renewal Term (as detined herein) but agrees that rates may not increase by more than ten percent (10%) during any Renewal Term. If circumstances require VOTOCAST to raise its rates more than ten percent (10%) during any Renewal Term, VOTOCAST will provide Licensee cost related supporting documentation to justify the rate increase. VOTOCAST will give Licensee at least thirty (30) days prior written notice of any rate changes during any Renewal Term. Notwithstanding the above rate increase restrictions, VOTOCAST may at anytime increase prices without notice if there are unexpected operational costs that require it.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a clause allowing VOTOCAST to increase prices without notice under certain circumstances ('unexpected operational costs'), directly contradicting the original clause that restricts rate increases to a maximum of ten percent (10%) during any Renewal Term and requires thirty (30) days prior written notice. This creates a clear in-text contradiction regarding price increase limitations, making the agreement ambiguous and potentially unenforceable. This adheres to the Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction definition where a clause is placed in multiple locations (same location here) with conflicting implications.
